page: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is a virtualized server instance powered by the QEMU hypervisor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 shared vCPUs based on the AMD EPYC-Rome processor family, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 160 GB SSD. It does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25% at 2548.38. Multi-core performance under stress-ng and Geekbench falls within the average range, scoring 20252.78 and 7324.0 respectively. Memory bandwidth is average, delivering 888.0 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 89.3 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance exhibits weak single-threaded gzip compression performance at 23.39 GB/sec, but maintains average speeds for bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput at 10.98 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning, it processes 1073.74 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M LLM, though performance drops to a weak 31.68 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the cx43 offers a budget-friendly resource profile suitable for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. The combination of strong single-core performance and average database operation metrics makes it well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and moderate database tasks. However, the shared CPU model and limited memory per core present a tradeoff for sustained high-compute or memory-intensive workloads.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server instance running on the QEMU hypervisor, featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance includes 160 GB of local SSD storage but lacks dedicated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain in the average tier. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU allocation, the cx43 represents a budget-friendly option for workloads that can tolerate shared resources. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, optimized for budget-friendly workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for balanced, general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database performance
No bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and budget-friendly workloads
